answer.text: Increase in input costs pushing up prices
Demand being more than available supply
Decrease in money supply by the central bank

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Demand-pull inflation occurs when demand exceeds available supply, pushing prices up. Cost-push inflation is caused by increases in input costs. **The Trap:** Input cost increase is cost-push, not demand-pull. Monetary tightening and reduced spending would reduce inflation. **Cross-Exam Context:** Inflation types tested in NISM Series XV and I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** During economic expansion, demand-pull inflation is common. During supply disruptions (like oil shocks), cost-push inflation dominates.
